name: install-and-mod
description: End-to-end orchestration of a from-scratch modded setup, run to a verified instance. Coordinates the whole journey — pick a real MC version + loader, create the instance, check isolation, install mods/packs, then verify the files landed — not any single step. Use when the user wants a new modded setup built for them ("装个版本","装模组","加mod","Fabric","Forge","NeoForge"); creating only the empty instance is create-instance, dropping mods into an existing instance is mods-install, a prebuilt pack is use-modpacks.
body:
# Install a version + add mods (orchestration)

Goal: take the user from "I want mods" to a working, verified instance, end-to-end — never hand
back manual download/install steps.

Flow:
1. `instance(action=list)` first — an existing instance may already fit; don't create a duplicate.
2. Get REAL options before asking anything: `search(action=game_versions)` for live MC versions,
   `search(action=mods, ...)` for each requested mod's real compatible builds and required loader.
   Never pick a version or mod build from training memory.
3. MC version and loader are concrete choices (Fabric for the Sodium/performance-mod family; Forge
   or NeoForge for big modpacks; Quilt only if the user asks for it) — bundle them into ONE `ask`
   call, options drawn from step 2. If the user hasn't named which mods they want, that part is
   open-ended: don't wrap it alone in `ask` — ask for names/keywords directly in your response
   text (or, if version/loader are ALSO still open, bundle it as a free-text sub-question alongside
   those single-choice questions in the same `ask` call — bundling 2+ sub-questions together is
   fine, a lone free-text question is not).
4. Create the instance — mechanics in `create-instance`.
5. Before installing anything, check isolation — mechanics in `set-isolation`. If OFF,
   mods/saves/config are shared across all versions; warn the user before installing
   version-specific content.
6. Install the chosen mods/resourcepacks/shaders into the instance just created — mods: mechanics
   in `mods-install`; resourcepacks/shaders: mechanics in
   `resourcepacks-and-shaders`. Don't mix loaders (no Fabric mod on a Forge instance or
   vice versa) and don't skip mod dependencies (e.g. Fabric API). If what the user actually wants
   is a modpack, that's a different flow — see the `use-modpacks` skill instead: a modpack always
   installs as its own brand-new instance, never merged into the one created in step 4.
7. Verify: `glob`/`read` the instance's `mods/` dir to confirm files landed, then tell the user
   it's ready and offer `game(action=launch)`.
